description_long: This object is a compact, dual-canister bomb with a robust, industrial design. It features two large cylindrical tanks mounted symmetrically on either side of a central rectangular body, which is reinforced with angular plating and a prominent handle at the top. The tanks are connected to the main body by sturdy brackets and pipes, suggesting a pressurized or chemical payload. The central section has a series of horizontal ridges and a pair of vertical rods, possibly for stabilization or mounting. The overall color scheme is dark gray and black, giving it a utilitarian, tactical appearance. No visible warning labels or timer displays are present, but the presence of pipes and possible switches implies a mechanical or remote detonation mechanism.
description: A dual-canister, industrial-style bomb with a central reinforced body, dark gray and black coloration, and prominent handle and piping.
description_view_0: Front view shows the handle, central body, and symmetrical canisters with connecting pipes and brackets.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the cylindrical shape of the canisters and the depth of the central body, with visible mounting brackets.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the horizontal ridges on the central body and the full structure of the connecting pipes and rods.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the canister profile and attachment details.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation likely initiated via mechanical switches or remote trigger, as suggested by the presence of pipes and possible control rods.
